back_translation: David and the commanders of the army set apart some of the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun to prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals. The list of those who did this work is as follows:
From the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah. These sons of Asaph were under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the king's command.

From the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six in all—under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the lyre to thank and praise the LORD.
From the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.
All these were sons of Heman the king's seer. God gave them to Heman according to the promise to exalt him; God gave Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

All these were under the direction of their fathers to make music in the house of the LORD with cymbals, harps, and lyres, for the service of the house of God. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the king's command.
Together with their relatives, who were trained and skillful in singing to the LORD, they numbered 288 in all.
They cast lots for their duties, old and young alike, teacher as well as pupil.
The first lot, which was for Asaph, fell to Joseph, with his sons and brothers—12 in all; the second to Gedaliah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the third to Zaccur, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the fourth to Izri, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the fifth to Nethaniah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the sixth to Bukkiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the seventh to Jesarelah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the eighth to Jeshaiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the ninth to Mattaniah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the tenth to Shimei, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the eleventh to Azarel, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the twelfth to Hashabiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the thirteenth to Shubael, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the fourteenth to Mattithiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the fifteenth to Jeremoth, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the sixteenth to Hananiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the seventeenth to Joshbekashah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the eighteenth to Hanani, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the nineteenth to Mallothi, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the twentieth to Eliathah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the twenty-first to Hothir, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the twenty-second to Giddalti, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
the twenty-third to Mahazioth, with his sons and brothers—12 in all;
and the twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
